description: Real capture (2026-07-09, SDK 0.3.206) of the NEW graceful-interrupt flow: a 12s foreground turn gets two queued messages, then POST /interrupt. Unlike the pre-receipt flow (sdk206-queued-message-interrupt, where the stream just STOPS), the container now calls the SDK interrupt control request first: the SDK emits real command_lifecycle cancelled frames for all three commands plus a full termination sequence (result subtype error_during_execution with terminal_reason aborted_tools — error-SHAPED but a deliberate stop — and a trailing idle), and the container appends synthetic command_lifecycle discarded frames for the two queued uuids its abort killed. Guards: lifecycle forwarding, interrupt classification (NO session_error for aborted_tools), and quiet settling.
